prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a oxim has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the oxim-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a oxim, a greeble, and a muk. The oxim is underneath the greeble. The greeble is to-the-left-of the muk. The oxim has a purple button on top, a blue button on side, and a green button on bottom. The greeble has a blue button on top and a purple button on bottom. The muk has a green button on top and a purple button on side. Pressing the purple button on the oxim activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a zop, a corp, and a mek. The corp is underneath the zop. The zop is to-the-left-of the mek. The zop has a purple button on top, a purple button on side, and a blue button on bottom. The corp has a purple button on top, a blue button on side, and a green button on bottom. The mek has a green button on top and a blue button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the oxim-analog, and which button activates it?
